RULING:

DEFENDANT KIMMAI LIEN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge James L. Warren, Reporter Christina Paxton CSR #1558. (301/JLW/RB-X)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2005 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Jan-13-2005 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Tony Vaughn.
Robert Shepard, Esq. for Plaintiff: Gopal Chopra, present, was sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendants: Kimmai Lien aka Kim Mai and Forda International Trading Inc., dba Kim Mai Jewelry.
Counsel to prepare a Proposed Judgment and submit to Dept. 218 for signature.

Jan. 13, 2005
Proposed Judgment rec'd in court and granted as follows:
Compensatory Damages $105,723.85
Punitive Damages $317,171.55
Costs $382.50 and Interest as provided by law.
Judgment signed by Comm. Kaplan.

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Dec-16-2004 Continued To Jan-06-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-03-2004 Continued To Feb-04-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Nov-18-2004 Continued To Dec-16-04 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Tony Vaughn.
Robert L. Shepard, Esq. for Plaintiff: Gopal Chopra, present, was sworn and examined.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court ordered this matter continued to Dec. 16, 2004 at 9:00 a.m. for proof of Notice of intent to seek punitive damages.
